explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A privacy notice describes contractual agreements with third parties for services such as analytics and advertising. It also details vendor management and data use for optimization.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This Uni-Select Privacy Notice, a third-party privacy policy, provides evidence for the **governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency** pillars. It supports governance and oversight by describing contractual agreements with third parties for services like analytics and advertising, and by detailing vendor management and data use for optimization. The policy addresses privacy and transparency by mentioning the use of AI/ML features for ad optimization based on past visits, explicit references to "inferences" and "predictions" for profiling, and user rights regarding automated decision-making and profiling.
relevant_sources.title: Uni-Select Privacy Notice

oversight.findings: A privacy notice describes contractual agreements with third parties for services and details vendor management and data use for optimization. A case study describes AI monitoring and performance display, alongside a human-in-the-loop escalation process for inspections.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This Uni-Select Privacy Notice, a third-party privacy policy, provides evidence for the **governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency** pillars. relevant_sources.summary: This third-party case study on LKQ's use of Groundlight AI provides evidence for the **oversight** and **transparency** pillars. It describes AI monitoring and performance display, along with a human-in-the-loop escalation process for inspections, indicating operational oversight mechanisms. The mention of AI capabilities and learning, while aspirational, also touches upon the transparency of the AI's function.
relevant_sources.title: LKQ Uses Groundlight AI for Quality Control and Inspection

privacy.findings: A privacy notice mentions the use of AI/ML features for ad optimization based on past visits. It also explicitly references "inferences" and "predictions" for profiling, and user rights regarding automated decision-making and profiling.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.findings: A privacy notice mentions the use of AI/ML features for ad optimization, explicitly referencing inferences and predictions for profiling, and user rights related to automated decision-making. Other sources describe AI capabilities and learning, and detail the application of AI computer vision technology for tasks like assessing vehicle damage, explaining its training and processing capacity.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar by detailing the application of Tractable's AI computer vision technology for assessing vehicle damage and identifying recyclable parts. The source explains the AI's capabilities, its training on millions of examples, and its daily processing capacity, offering insight into the system's development and scale. While it highlights the AI's function and performance, it does not include details on operational or policy execution.
relevant_sources.title: LKQ Partners with Tractable for AI-Powered Auto Parts Recycling

summary.overall_findings: LKQ's public disclosures indicate that a privacy notice mentions the use of AI/ML features for ad optimization based on past visits, addressing aspects of transparency and privacy. These are among 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars for which documented evidence exists, with all covered pillars addressed at the policy level. For instance, materials related to oversight and governance describe contractual agreements with third parties for services like analytics and advertising. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 1 additional pillar. This assessment draws on 8 publicly available sources.
